title: Brandon Theriault
text:
Brandon Theriault


Senior People Research Scientist  ·  People Analytics & AI Integration


I design and build enterprise analytics systems at the intersection of workforce science and AI — translating people data into decisions that affect how organizations hire, develop, and retain talent. My background spans I/O psychology, machine learning, and full-stack data engineering, with experience scaling research platforms across workforces of 1M+ employees.


Formerly at Block, Inc. (Square, Cash App, TIDAL, Afterpay), where I lead many aspects of people analytics research and maintain production systems processing. My team was affected by the Feb 2026 layoffs. Previously at Amazon, where I directed an employee experience research portfolio and co-architected Amazon Connections — a daily pulse platform reaching 500,000+ frontline associates.


📍 Seattle, WA  ·  b5theriault@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/b5theriault




12+ Years Experience


1M+ Employees Reached


10+ Live Production Systems


CRAN R Package Maintainer



Research Focus

Workforce intelligence  ·  AI tool adoption and performance  ·  Attrition modeling and retention risk  ·  Organizational network analysis  ·  NLP for employee feedback  ·  Causal inference in people data  ·  Predictive modeling with XGBoost & CatBoost

Selected Work




AI Tool Adoption & Performance


Correlational and regression analysis linking AI tool usage to calibrated performance ratings across H1 and H2 2025.

See all work samples →



Predictive Modeling (XGBoost + CatBoost)


Multi-model approach to predicting performance ratings from activity signals — with feature importance, confusion matrices, and full methodology appendix.

See all work samples →



Termination & Retention Analysis


Survival analysis, exit survey heatmaps, and high-risk departure profiles — from deep dive to executive summary format.

See all work samples →



Open Source

gooseR (CRAN) — R package bridging AI assistants with R development environments. Includes persistent memory management, session tracking, bulk operations, and sub-100ms streaming latency. Reduces AI-assisted coding friction by 10–20×.
→ github.com/blockbtheriault/gooseR

title: Brandon Theriault
text:
Brandon Theriault


Seattle, WA  ·  b5theriault@gmail.com  ·  linkedin.com/in/b5theriault



Strategic People Research Scientist and AI integration leader with 12+ years of experience transforming workforce data into actionable commercial strategies. Expert in scaling advanced analytics, LLM workflows, and human-centered design across highly complex, matrixed environments to drive business-critical decisions. Proven track record of owning enterprise-wide platforms, advising cross-functional leadership, and delivering millions in cost savings for workforces of 1M+ employees.



Professional Experience


Block, Inc.  ·  Senior People Research Scientist August 2024 – Present  ·  Seattle, WA


Square, Cash App, TIDAL, and Afterpay · 13,000+ employees · 10+ live production deployments


Scope of Ownership: Direct ownership of enterprise-wide analytics platforms and major HR architecture redesigns. Completely revamped Block’s job architecture — analysed 10,425 employees across 1,058 job codes to propose a unified Specialist/Generalist/Associate framework. Built the Elevate Matching Algorithm v4.0, generating 1,140 mentorship matches across 100+ skill dimensions with 98.3% sponsor coverage and a 95% reduction in processing time.
Platforms & Infrastructure: Architect of “Feedback Fusion,” an NLP engine processing 121,000+ records with 96.4% sentiment accuracy. Reduced query latency by 95% (12 hours → 52 minutes) via Snowflake/PostgreSQL pipeline automation and CTE batch processing. Built a company-wide activity dataset integrating 13,138 employees across 71 dimensions and six AI tools.
Predictive Modelling: Engineered high-precision models including XGBoost (0.985 AUC) for promotion forecasting and causal inference analysis for specialised workforce retention — identifying a critical “tenure cliff” and delivering a 500% ROI. Built a Sales AE Commitment Score analysing 1,067 Account Executives, quantifying a 49% performance gap between quartiles.
Cross-Functional Influence: Strategic advisor to Talent Acquisition, HR Ops, Sales, and Executive Leadership. AI integration leadership delivered $67K+ in cost savings and 1,300+ hours in efficiency gains. Designed organisational influence maps using a 6-factor scoring algorithm across 10,000+ employees to identify succession risks and high-impact ICs.
Open Source: Creator and maintainer of gooseR (CRAN) — an R package bridging AI assistants with R development environments, reducing AI-assisted coding friction by 10–20× via intelligent caching and sub-100ms streaming latency.


Amazon.com, Inc.  ·  Senior Research Scientist October 2017 – July 2023  ·  Seattle, WA


Promoted from Research Scientist (February 2015 – October 2017) · 1M+ employees globally


Scope of Ownership: Managed a cross-functional team of 8 researchers specialising in AI/ML applications and advanced people analytics. Owned research enabling AWS Infrastructure expansion from 3 to 80 sites in 6 months. Co-architected Amazon Connections, a daily pulse survey platform reaching 500,000+ frontline employees.
Cross-Functional Influence: Directly influenced VP-level strategic decisions on workforce planning, employee engagement, and talent placement across highly complex global logistics and tech environments.
Commercial Impact: Launched the U.S. Frontline Associate Exit Survey & Dashboard, reducing turnover by 15% and generating $10M in annual savings. Developed a preference-based placement algorithm using a Random Forest ML model trained on heuristics-based surveys and process-path performance metrics, decreasing early attrition by 20%. Improved hiring efficiency by 30% through predictive workforce planning models.


SAP Inc.  ·  Global HR Early Talent Team August 2011 – January 2015  ·  Newtown Square, PA


PMO, HRBP, and Research Analyst capacities · 4 Canadian business units · 500+ employees


Selected for the high-potential Global HR Early Talent program, partnering directly with company leaders across functions.
Led global research that streamlined organisational restructuring, reducing process time by 80% ($2M annual savings).
Increased diversity representation by 25% through targeted workforce analytics projects. Orchestrated company-wide People & Diversity Week for 10,000+ participants.



Technical Skills & Open Source



Programming R (Expert · CRAN Maintainer), Python (Advanced), SQL, Snowflake, PostgreSQL


Platforms Git/GitHub, AWS, Qualtrics, Tableau, Power BI, Jira


ML Methods XGBoost, Random Forest, CatBoost, Neural Networks, K-NN, Clustering


Stats Methods Causal Inference, Psychometrics, SEM, IRT, Factor Analysis, Survival Analysis


Research Experimental Design, A/B Testing, Survey Design, NLP, Predictive Modelling


Visualisation ggplot2, Plotly, D3.js, Shiny, Tableau, Adobe Creative Suite



gooseR (CRAN)  ·  Creator & Maintainer  ·  github.com/b5theriault/gooseR R package bridging AI assistants with R development environments. Persistent memory management, session tracking, bulk operations, and markdown rendering for AI output. 20,000+ potential users.



Education


Master of Science — Industrial-Organisational Psychology West Chester University, West Chester, PA  ·  2012 Thesis: Virtual Teamwork: Optimising Remote Collaboration in Global Organisations


Bachelor of Science — Behavioral Psychology Western Michigan University, Kalamazoo, MI  ·  2009  ·  Lee Honors College Minor: Human Resource Management

title: Work Samples
text:
Each report below is a self-contained interactive HTML file — click to open in a new tab. Code is hidden by default; toggle via the Code button in each report. All underlying data is synthetic and does not represent real individuals or organisations.




AI Tool Adoption & Performance Analysis


R ggplot2 Pearson Correlation OLS Regression plotly


Links AI tool usage telemetry (Copilot, Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT) to calibrated H1 and H2 2025 performance ratings at the individual level. Includes department-level adoption rate analysis with simulated realistic variation, bivariate and controlled regression models, and an interactive employee data table.

View Report →



Org-Wide Activity Analysis & Predictive Modeling


R Python XGBoost CatBoost Correlation Matrix Feature Importance


Integrates Slack messaging, meeting load, AI adoption, and engagement signals with H2 performance ratings. Features dual predictive models — XGBoost trained natively in R and CatBoost trained in Python with native categorical handling. Full methodology appendix documents all variables, engineered features, and statistical methods.

View Report →



Termination Deep Dive


R Survival Analysis Kaplan-Meier Exit Survey NLP Log-Rank Test


Examines 2025 departure patterns by regrettability, department, and tenure. Includes Kaplan-Meier survival curves with 95% CIs and log-rank testing, exit survey satisfaction heatmaps, high-risk departure profiles, and evidence-based retention intervention priorities across three time horizons.

View Report →



Termination Executive Summary


R Executive Communication Dynamic Inline R Custom CSS


CEO-facing one-page summary of 2025 departure trends. KPI cards populated with live R values, root cause analysis with dynamic percentages, department impact table, and prioritised action horizons across Weeks 1–2, Month 1, and Quarter 1.

View Report →



H2 2025 Performance Calibration Report


R ggplot2 DT Pre/Post Analysis Exit Construct Validity


Analyses pre- and post-calibration rating distributions across functions, levels, job profiles, and promotion cohorts. Validates calibration quality via exit population construct validity check — departed employees should skew toward lower ratings, and they do.

View Report →



People Ops Ticket & Chatbot Dashboard


R plotly DT Wilcoxon Test Service Analytics


Tracks HR service delivery across ticket resolution time, satisfaction scores by category, and AI chatbot vs. human agent effectiveness. Includes a channel-level satisfaction comparison with Wilcoxon rank-sum testing and a footnote on variance vs. mean as a service quality signal.

View Report →
